file_name: Apollo Endosurgery - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.2"Certificate of Conformance" or "COC" shall mean a document prepared by ESTABLISHMENT containing at a minimum: product name, Lot (defined below) number, lot quantity and a statement indicating compliance to all product specifications. Each COC shall be signature approved by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance department.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2"Certificate of Conformance" or "COC" shall mean a document prepared by ESTABLISHMENT containing at a minimum: product name and lot quantity.
perturbation.explanation: By removing key requirements for a Certificate of Conformance (COC), namely the 'Lot number' and 'a statement indicating compliance to all product specifications', and the fact that it needs to be approved by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance Department. This change introduces uncertainty and lowers quality assurance standards. In particular Section 7.9 refers to quality control testing in accordance with the specifications for the release of each Lot of Product to APOLLO, which will not longer be ensured by a Certificate of Conformance with all product specifications. Therefore, this omission creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.9Control Testing. ESTABLISHMENT shall perform quality control testing in accordance with the Specifications for release of each Lot of Product to APOLLO. Quality control testing shall include testing associated with the production of the Product, including, but not limited to, incoming component and raw material testing, in process testing, and final release testing as agreed upon from time to time between APOLLO and ESTABLISHMENT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes key requirements from the definition of "Certificate of Conformance" (COC), specifically 'Lot number', 'a statement indicating compliance to all product specifications', and the requirement for approval by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance department. This directly contradicts Section 7.9, which mandates that ESTABLISHMENT perform quality control testing according to specifications for each Lot of Product released to APOLLO. Without the COC including a statement of compliance to specifications and Lot number, it is no longer possible to ensure compliance with Section 7.9, therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text:
5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a monthly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast to allow for visibility into expected future demands. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated monthly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and is not a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast, except as described below. The quantities of Product forecasted for the initial three (3) months of each updated rolling Forecast shall represent a binding obligation of Apollo to purchase from ESTABLISHMENT, and of ESTABLISHMENT to manufacture and supply to APOLLO, such quantities of Product.
-5- ESTABLISHMENT shall, at all times during the Term, maintain an inventory of raw materials and components sufficient to manufacture the binding obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a quarterly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated quarterly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and is not a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the monthly basis forecast and making it quarterly, as well as, removing the binding obligation description, there is an in-text contradiction. In particular, the last sentence of the original excerpt states: 'ESTABLISHMENT shall, at all times during the Term, maintain an inventory of raw materials and components sufficient to manufacture the binding obligations.' which are not binding any more. This will create uncertainty in the enforceability of inventory levels.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a quarterly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated quarterly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and is not a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a binding obligation for Apollo to purchase quantities of product for the first three months of each rolling forecast, and required ESTABLISHMENT to maintain sufficient inventory to meet these binding obligations. The changed text removes the monthly forecast basis and the binding obligation clause. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text and it removes the binding obligation language while, as described in the explanation, ESTABLISHMENT is still required to maintain inventory. Since the Forecast is not a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast based on the Changed text, then this creates a contradiction since ESTABLISHMENT is required to maintain inventory to manufacture binding obligations, which no longer exist.

perturbation.original_text: ESTABLISHMENT warrants that the Facilities have been periodically inspected by its Notified Body's representatives and auditors and/or any other required government agency and are in good standing with said governmental agencies, are fully compliant with ISO 13485:2012 and that all employees working on the Product whose responsibilities involve work which must be performed under ISO 13485:2012 standards have been properly trained in the requirements of those standards. ESTABLISHMENT additionally warrants that the Facilities hold all necessary licenses and permits from applicable local, national, and European regulatory bodies, required for the manufacture and testing of the Product and that all such licenses and permits are in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: ESTABLISHMENT warrants that the Facilities are in good standing with governmental agencies and that all employees working on the Product whose responsibilities involve work have been properly trained. ESTABLISHMENT additionally warrants that the Facilities hold all necessary licenses and permits from applicable regulatory bodies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'have been periodically inspected by its Notified Body's representatives and auditors and/or any other required government agency', 'are fully compliant with ISO 13485:2012' and 'work which must be performed under ISO 13485:2012 standards', there is an in-text contradiction. In particular, Section 7.2 states: 'Establishment shall maintain and follow a quality control and testing program consistent with the Product Specifications, ISO 13485:2012, Applicable Laws and quality system requirements communicated in writing by APOLLO from time to time', which no longer ensured by the ESTABLISHMENT representations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2Quality Control. Establishment shall maintain and follow a quality control and testing program consistent with the Product Specifications, ISO 13485:2012, Applicable Laws and quality system requirements communicated in writing by APOLLO from time to time -7- (the "Quality Control Procedures"). All Product supplied to APOLLO hereunder shall be manufactured in compliance with ISO 13485:2012 and all other applicable requirements of Regulatory Authorities, and in compliance with all other Applicable Laws (collectively, "Regulatory Standards"). At all times the Products shall be manufactured in an ISO Class 7 Clean Room, unless otherwise set forth in an amendment to this Agreement or the Exhibits hereto signed by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit warranty that the facilities are fully compliant with ISO 13485:2012. Section 7.2 requires ESTABLISHMENT to maintain a quality control and testing program consistent with ISO 13485:2012. By removing the warranty of compliance, the changed text creates a contradiction because ESTABLISHMENT no longer explicitly warrants it is following ISO 13485:2012 as mandated in Section 7.2. This aligns with 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' where essential language is removed, contradicting other obligations.
